description: Intl.Segmenter Latin1/two-byte char buffer type confusion after string atomization. CVE-2025-0241 (sec-moderate, MFSA 2025-01, Firefox 134). SegmentsObject cached the underlying JS string's Latin1 bit at construction time via getString()->hasLatin1Chars(). Bug 1881995's atom-ref string mechanism allows a two-byte JSString to be re-pointed at a freshly created Latin1 atom (e.g., when used as a property key in an object literal), changing the actual character buffer encoding without notifying SegmentsObject. Subsequent reads of the segment's characters dispatched on the cached two-byte path while the buffer was actually Latin1, producing type-confused reads and a debug MOZ_ASSERT failure at js/src/builtin/intl/Segmenter.h:141. The fix (commit 6a1bdb80cb57384cd915ae2e27d3363a13ecb6f9 by Jan de Mooij, 2024-11-27, r=anba) introduces SegmentsObject::hasLatin1StringChars() that queries the segment's actual character buffer rather than the parent JS string. Reporter: Nils Bars.

fixes.description: Bug 1933023 - Check whether the segment's chars are Latin1
